checks.details: PHP line 152 correctly uses empty string fallback for decorative images (alt="") instead of post title. TSX line 334 matches.
PHP line 114 correctly includes aria-label with format 'buttonLabel: postTitle' for screen reader context
Proper heading hierarchy (h2 for regular, h3 for small), semantic section wrapper, no layout tables

accessibility.patternCompliance: skipped - no WAI-ARIA patterns apply (content display block with standard link)

markupDuplication.details: No responsive duplication detected. Single DOM structure with CSS-only responsive behavior

tsxPhpSync.details: Major sync issue: PHP file uses manual attribute extraction instead of auto-extracted variables

issues.description: PHP manually extracts attributes from $attributes array
issues.details: The render_callback in inc/helpers/blocks.php already calls extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP) on line 20, making all attributes available as local variables. Manual extraction is redundant and violates the theme convention.
issues.fix: Remove lines 10-27 and add @var docblock at top of file listing all attribute variables to prevent PHP_CodeSniffer warnings for camelCase variables

issues.description: Missing @var docblock for auto-extracted attribute variables
issues.details: PHP_CodeSniffer warns about camelCase variables like $isReversed, $reverseRows, $darkMode, $buttonLabel, $headingSize, $eyebrow. The file needs a @var docblock documenting these auto-extracted attribute variables.
issues.fix: Add @var annotations for all camelCase attribute variables in the file header docblock

structureSync.details: HTML structure matches between TSX (lines 186-346) and PHP (lines 40-161). Element order, classes, and conditional rendering patterns are synchronized.

innerBlocks.reason: Block does not support inner blocks

specCompliance.details: Test page has 10 block instances matching the 10 variations specified in planning spec. All variations use correct self-closing block markup format and are wrapped in DemoContainer blocks with descriptive titles.

issues.description: PHP manually extracts attributes from $attributes array instead of using auto-extracted variables
issues.details: The render_callback in inc/helpers/blocks.php already calls extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP) on line 20, making all attributes available as local variables ($selectedPost, $isReversed, $reverseRows, $darkMode, $buttonLabel, $headingSize, $eyebrow). Manual extraction with $var = $attributes['key'] ?? default is redundant and violates theme conventions.
issues.fix: Remove lines 10-27: $selectedPost = $attributes['selectedPost'] ?? null; through $eyebrow = $attributes['eyebrow'] ?? ''; The variables are already available from auto-extraction. If defaults are needed, use null coalescing in usage: <?php if ( ! empty( $eyebrow ?? '' ) ) : ?>
issues.impact: Code duplication, violates theme convention, makes code harder to maintain. Not a runtime bug but incorrect pattern.

issues.description: Missing @var docblock for auto-extracted camelCase attribute variables
issues.details: PHP_CodeSniffer warns about camelCase variables ($isReversed, $reverseRows, $darkMode, $buttonLabel, $headingSize, $eyebrow) because WordPress coding standards prefer snake_case. These warnings are expected for block attributes (which use camelCase from JavaScript), but the file needs @var annotations to document that these variables come from auto-extracted block attributes.
issues.fix:
Add to the file header docblock after line 8:
 * Available variables (auto-extracted from $attributes):
 * @var int $selectedPost
 * @var bool $isReversed
 * @var bool $reverseRows
 * @var bool $darkMode
 * @var string $buttonLabel
 * @var string $headingSize
 * @var string $eyebrow
 *
 * Also available:
 * @var WP_Block $block Block instance
 * @var string $content Block content
issues.impact: PHP_CodeSniffer warnings for every use of camelCase variables. Makes linting output noisy.

issues.details: Line 70 declares const postPermalink = post?.link || '#'; but the variable is never used. The TSX editor preview doesn't render functional links (uses span.btn-primary instead of anchor), so the permalink is not needed in the editor.
issues.fix: Remove line 70: const postPermalink = post?.link || '#'; (or prefix with underscore if keeping for documentation: const _postPermalink)
issues.impact: Lint error, no runtime impact. Auto-fixable.

issues.description: Experimental WordPress component warnings for ToggleGroupControl
issues.details: Lines 12-13 import __experimentalToggleGroupControl and __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption. These are experimental WordPress components but are the standard recommended controls for this UI pattern (selecting between 'small' and 'regular' heading size).
issues.fix: No action needed - this is acceptable. When WordPress stabilizes these components, the imports can be updated.
issues.impact: Lint warnings only. No runtime impact. These components are stable in practice despite the experimental naming.

All blocks wrapped in DemoContainer with descriptive titles

Accessibility fixes correctly applied: CTA links have aria-label with post title context
TSX and PHP HTML structure synchronized
Post excerpt uses wp_kses_post() to prevent double-escaping
Semantic HTML with proper heading hierarchy (h2/h3)
recommendations: Fix the manual attribute extraction in PHP (FQA-001) - this is a major convention violation that should be addressed before production
Add @var docblock annotations (FQA-002) to suppress PHP_CodeSniffer warnings for camelCase attribute variables
Remove unused postPermalink variable in TSX (FQA-003) to eliminate lint error
performanceNotes: Featured images use wp_get_attachment_image() which includes srcset and sizes attributes for responsive images
No N+1 query issues - block fetches a single post by ID
Images use 'full' size with srcset as recommended
